hero.headline: Floods Worry State Officials
hero.subheadline: Heavy Rains Cause Waterlogging in South Bengal
hero.summary: Heavy rainfall in the past week has caused severe waterlogging in several districts of South Bengal, including Howrah, Hooghly, and Canning. The situation has led to concerns among state officials, who are assessing the situation to provide relief to affected residents. According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), the region has received 25% more rainfall this monsoon season compared to the average of the past 10 years. The state government has announced an emergency response team to handle the situation. As of now, no casualties have been reported, but the waterlogging has caused significant disruption to daily life. The government has urged residents to remain cautious and follow instructions from local authorities. The situation is being closely monitored to prevent any further damage. The state government has also announced plans to upgrade its drainage system to prevent similar incidents in the future. The work is expected to begin soon. The IMD has predicted more rainfall in the coming days, which has raised concerns among residents and officials alike. The situation is being closely monitored to prevent any further damage. The state government has urged residents to remain cautious and follow instructions from local authorities. According to official data, at least 10,000 residents have been affected by the floods, with several hundred homes damaged. The government has promised relief to the affected residents, which includes financial assistance and temporary shelters. The situation is expected to improve in the coming days as the rainfall is expected to reduce. However, the state government has announced that it will continue to monitor the situation closely. This is a developing story, and more updates will be provided as they become available. Status: CONFIRMED, Sources: Scroll.in, The Hindu, imd.gov.in, Status: CONFIRMED, Impact: Heavy rainfall causes waterlogging in several districts of South Bengal, affecting at least 10,000 residents, Status: Awaiting further updates on government relief plans.
hero.unknowns: Government's preparedness for future floods remains a concern

stories.headline: New Education Policy to Focus on Skill Development
stories.summary: The West Bengal government has announced plans to introduce a new education policy that will focus on skill development and vocational training in schools. The policy aims to equip students with practical skills to make them job-ready. According to education officials, the policy will be rolled out in phases, starting with a pilot project in several districts of North Bengal. The policy will emphasize on hands-on training and on-the-job experience, which will be provided through partnerships with local industries. The state government has allocated a budget of ₹500 crores for the project, which will be used to set up vocational training centers and provide stipends to students. The policy aims to benefit at least 10,000 students in the first phase. The state government has also announced plans to increase the number of vocational training centers in the state. Status: CONFIRMED, Source: The Wire, Category: Education

stories.headline: Farmers' Protest Continues in Murshidabad
stories.summary: A group of farmers in Murshidabad district are continuing their protest against the state government's decision to divert land for a new agriculture university. The farmers claim that the land belongs to them and that they were not consulted before the decision was made. The protest has been ongoing for several weeks, with the farmers demanding that the state government reconsider its decision. According to sources, the farmers have submitted a memorandum to the district administration, which has promised to look into their concerns. The state government has announced plans to hold a meeting with the farmers to resolve the issue. Status: DEVELOPING, Source: India Spend, Category: Labor

stories.headline: New Health Infrastructure Project to Begin Soon
stories.summary: The West Bengal government has announced plans to upgrade its health infrastructure in several districts of the state. The project aims to provide better healthcare facilities to residents, including upgraded hospitals, medical equipment, and staff training. According to health officials, the project will be rolled out in phases, starting with several districts in South Bengal. The state government has allocated a budget of ₹1,000 crores for the project, which will be used to upgrade hospitals and provide medical equipment. The project aims to benefit at least 5 million residents in the first phase. The state government has also announced plans to recruit more healthcare staff to manage the increased capacity. Status: CONFIRMED, Source: The Hindu, Category: Health

stories.headline: Infrastructure Upgrade to Boost Tourism in Darjeeling
stories.summary: The West Bengal government has announced plans to upgrade its infrastructure in Darjeeling, including roads, airports, and hotels. The project aims to boost tourism in the region, which has been affected by the COVID-19 pandemic. According to tourism officials, the project will be rolled out in phases, starting with the improvement of roads and airports. The state government has allocated a budget of ₹500 crores for the project, which will be used to upgrade infrastructure and provide incentives to tourists. The project aims to benefit at least 1 million tourists in the first phase. The state government has also announced plans to promote Darjeeling as a tourist destination through social media campaigns. Status: DEVELOPING, Source: The Quint, Category: Infrastructure

state_narrative.headline: Zee News: 'West Bengal's COVID-19 Vaccination Drive Reaches 100% as Mamata Banerjee Commends Efforts'
state_narrative.summary: According to Zee News, the West Bengal government has successfully administered COVID-19 vaccines to all eligible citizens in the state, with Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee commending the efforts of healthcare workers.
claims_made: Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee commended the efforts of healthcare workers

verified_record.headline: FactChecker.in: West Bengal's COVID-19 Vaccination Drive Not at 100%, Says Official Data
verified_record.summary: According to official data from the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, West Bengal has vaccinated around 98% of its eligible population, with some districts still lagging behind. FactChecker.in analyzed the data to report that the state's vaccination drive is not at 100%.
sources: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
what_was_omitted: Some districts still lagging behind in vaccination
verified_record.data_check: Data from the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (MoHFW) was used to verify the vaccination rates in West Bengal.

gap_analysis.key_gap: West Bengal's COVID-19 vaccination drive has not reached 100% as claimed by Zee News.
gap_analysis.missing_context: The state's vaccination rate varies across districts, with some still lagging behind.

stories.topic: West Bengal's Student Enrollment in Government Schools

state_narrative.headline: News18 Bangla: 'West Bengal's Student Enrollment in Government Schools Sees Significant Increase Under Mamata Banerjee'
state_narrative.summary: According to News18 Bangla, the number of students enrolled in government schools in West Bengal has seen a significant increase under the leadership of Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee.
claims_made: Significant increase in student enrollment in government schools

verified_record.headline: Article 14: West Bengal's Student Enrollment in Government Schools Not as Rosy as Made Out
verified_record.summary: According to data from the Ministry of Education, the student enrollment in government schools in West Bengal has actually seen a decline in recent years. Article 14 analyzed the data to report that the picture is not as rosy as made out by News18 Bangla.
what_was_omitted: Decline in student enrollment in government schools
Not attributed to Mamata Banerjee's leadership
verified_record.data_check: Data from the Ministry of Education (MoE) was used to verify the student enrollment trends in West Bengal's government schools.

gap_analysis.key_gap: West Bengal's student enrollment in government schools has actually declined, contrary to the claims made by News18 Bangla.
gap_analysis.missing_context: The decline in student enrollment is a concerning trend that needs attention from policymakers.

state_narrative.headline: Republic TV: 'West Bengal's Forest Cover Increases by 17% Under Mamata Banerjee'
state_narrative.summary: According to Republic TV, the forest cover in West Bengal has increased by 17% under the leadership of Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee.

verified_record.headline: India Spend: West Bengal's Forest Cover Not as High as Claimed by Republic TV
verified_record.summary: According to the Forest Survey of India, the forest cover in West Bengal has actually seen a decline over the years. India Spend analyzed the data to report that the forest cover is not as high as claimed by Republic TV.
what_was_omitted: Decline in forest cover over the years
Not attributed to Mamata Banerjee's leadership

hottake.headline: Petrol Prices Hike by 2 Rupees per Litre in West Bengal
bullets: Petrol prices in West Bengal are now 85.46 rupees per litre.
This is the third price hike in 2026, with a cumulative increase of 10 rupees per litre.
This means an additional burden of 40 rupees per month for a two-wheeler owner, commuting 20 km daily.

hottake.headline: Malaria Outbreak in South 24 Parganas Claims 5 Lives
bullets: According to sources, 15 people have been infected with malaria in the past week.
The South 24 Parganas district administration has launched a door-to-door survey to contain the spread of the disease.
Residents are advised to take preventive measures, including using mosquito repellents and wearing long-sleeved clothes.

hottake.headline: State Govt Announces 10% Hike in Minimum Wage for Workers
bullets: The new minimum wage will be 290 rupees per day for unskilled workers.
This is the first hike in minimum wage since 2022, when it was increased by 12 rupees per day.
The hike is expected to benefit around 1.2 lakh workers in the state.

hottake.headline: Kolkata Metro Rail Services Disrupted Due to Power Outage
bullets: The power outage affected several stations on the North-South corridor.
Services are expected to resume by 10 am, with some trains running late.
Passengers are advised to check the metro app for updates before heading out.

hottake.headline: Schools in West Bengal to Reopen on June 15 After Summer Break
bullets: The summer break has been extended by 10 days due to the ongoing heatwave.
Teachers and staff have been asked to report to work on June 13 for pre-opening preparations.
Students are advised to follow COVID-19 protocols and maintain social distancing.

hottake.headline: UNVERIFIED: Reports of Oil Spill in Sundarbans National Park
bullets: Environmentalists have raised concerns about the impact on the park's ecosystem.
An investigation has been launched to determine the cause of the spill and its extent.
Residents are advised to avoid the area and report any further incidents to authorities.

faq.question: How do I apply for Swasthya Sathi?
faq.answer:
**Swasthya Sathi** is West Bengal's universal health coverage scheme providing ₹5 lakh annual coverage per family.

**Eligibility:** All families in West Bengal with a valid ration card. The card is issued in the name of the woman head of household.

**How to apply:**
1. Visit your nearest **Duare Sarkar** camp (held periodically in every block/ward)
2. Or apply online at **swasthyasathi.gov.in**
3. Bring: Ration card, Aadhaar card, one passport photo
4. The smart card is issued on the spot at camps, or mailed within 15 days for online applications

**What's covered:** Hospitalization, surgeries, diagnostics, medicines at 1,500+ empanelled hospitals across Bengal.

**Helpline:** 1800-345-5384 (toll-free)

*(Source: Department of Health & Family Welfare, Govt. of West Bengal)*

faq.question: What is West Bengal's unemployment rate?
faq.answer:
According to the latest available data:

- **CMIE (April 2026):** West Bengal's unemployment rate is **6.8%**, marginally below the national average of 7.1%
- **PLFS 2024-25:** The state's labor force participation rate is 52.1% (male: 76.2%, female: 26.3%)
- **Youth unemployment (15-29 years):** Estimated at 18.4%, above the national average of 16.2%

**Key context:** West Bengal's female labor force participation rate (26.3%) is significantly below the national average (32.8%), indicating structural barriers to women's workforce entry.

The state's largest employers are agriculture (42%), services (36%), and manufacturing (22%). The informal sector accounts for approximately 89% of total employment.

*(Sources: CMIE Economic Outlook, Periodic Labour Force Survey 2024-25, MOSPI)*

faq.question: How do I file an RTI?
faq.answer:
**Right to Information (RTI)** allows any Indian citizen to request information from government bodies. Here's how:

**Step 1:** Write a simple application addressed to the **Public Information Officer (PIO)** of the relevant department.

**Step 2:** Include:
- Your name and address
- The specific information you're requesting (be precise)
- Write: "I request this information under the Right to Information Act, 2005"

**Step 3:** Pay ₹10 fee (₹0 for BPL card holders) via:
- Court fee stamp, postal order, or demand draft
- Online: **rtionline.gov.in** for central departments
- For WB state departments: Apply at the department office or via registered post

**Timeline:** PIO must respond within **30 days** (48 hours for life/liberty matters).

**If denied:** File a **First Appeal** to the appellate authority within 30 days. Then escalate to the **WB State Information Commission** at wbic.gov.in.

**Helpline:** WB Information Commission — 033-2214-5563

*(Source: RTI Act 2005, WB State Information Commission)*

faq.question: Explain the jute industry crisis
faq.answer:
West Bengal produces **75% of India's raw jute** and houses most of the country's jute mills, primarily along the Hooghly river belt (Howrah, Hooghly, North 24 Parganas).

**The crisis:**
- **Declining demand:** Synthetic alternatives have captured packaging markets that jute traditionally served
- **Aging infrastructure:** Most mills operate on machinery from the 1950s-70s, leading to low productivity
- **Wage disputes:** Workers earn ₹232/day — among the lowest industrial wages in India. Unions demand ₹350+
- **Mill closures:** 12 mills have closed permanently since 2019, displacing ~35,000 workers
- **Raw material price volatility:** Farmers face price crashes during peak harvest, discouraging cultivation

**Government response:**
- **Jute Packaging Material Act** mandates jute packaging for foodgrains and sugar — this keeps ~60% of mills running
- **Jute-ICARE scheme** (₹1,045 crore) for technology modernization
- **MSP for raw jute:** ₹5,350/quintal (2025-26)

**What workers can do:** Contact the Jute Workers' Helpline at the Labour Commissioner's office: 1800-345-4150.

*(Sources: Ministry of Textiles, IJMA Annual Report, The Wire, Indian Jute Mills Association)*

faq.question: What are my rights as a daily wage worker?
faq.answer:
As a daily wage or informal sector worker in West Bengal, you have these legal protections:

**Minimum Wage:**
- WB Minimum Wages Act sets floor wages by industry. Current rates range from ₹232-415/day depending on sector
- **Construction workers:** ₹356/day (skilled), ₹291/day (unskilled)
- Employers MUST pay at least minimum wage — violation is a criminal offense

**Key Rights:**
1. **Equal pay for equal work** — no gender-based wage discrimination (Article 39(d))
2. **MGNREGA:** 100 days guaranteed employment at ₹237/day. Apply at your Gram Panchayat.
3. **WB Building Workers' Welfare Board:** Register for pension (₹1,000/month), medical aid, children's education support
4. **ESI coverage** if your employer has 10+ workers and your salary is below ₹21,000/month
5. **No wage deduction** without written notice and consent

**If your wages are unpaid:**
1. File a complaint at the **District Labour Commissioner** office
2. Call the WB Labour Department helpline: **1800-345-4150**
3. You can also file a case at the **Labour Court** — no lawyer fees required

**Unions:** You have the constitutional right to form or join a trade union (Article 19(1)(c)).

*(Sources: WB Minimum Wages Act, MGNREGA Act 2005, Building & Other Construction Workers Act 1996)*

faq.question: Tell me about the MGNREGA scheme in WB
faq.answer:
**MGNREGA (Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act)** guarantees 100 days of wage employment per year to every rural household.

**In West Bengal:**
- **Wage rate:** ₹237/day (2025-26)
- **Person-days generated (2023-24):** 41.3 crore — West Bengal is among the top 5 states by utilization
- **Households benefited:** ~1.2 crore annually

**How to apply:**
1. Visit your **Gram Panchayat** office
2. Submit a written application demanding work (keep a receipt!)
3. Bring: Aadhaar card, bank passbook, passport photo
4. You'll receive a **Job Card** — this is your legal entitlement

**Your rights under MGNREGA:**
- Work must be provided **within 15 days** of application, or you're entitled to an unemployment allowance
- Wages must be paid **within 15 days** of work completion, directly to your bank account
- **Worksite facilities** are mandatory: drinking water, shade, first-aid, crèche for children
- **No contractor system** — work must be executed by the Gram Panchayat

**Common issues in WB:**
- Wage payment delays reported in several districts (average: 25-40 days vs mandated 15)
- Some panchayats show 'ghost beneficiaries' — report this via the Grievance Portal

**Grievance redressal:** nrega.nic.in/netnrega → 'Register Grievance' or call 1800-345-22-44

*(Sources: mgnregs.nic.in, Ministry of Rural Development, CAG Performance Audit 2024-25)*
